<!-- GENERATED by cmd/probe/compat; regenerate with `make compat-report`. Do not edit by hand. -->
# Broker compatibility report

This report is the published result of the broker-compatibility suite (Q191).
The suite exercises the GitHub Actions broker wire protocol and data contracts
end to end and asserts conformance to every documented contract in
[docs/design/03-api-contracts.md](../design/03-api-contracts.md). It exists to
confirm full broker compatibility before the v2beta1 API shape is frozen, so a
silent wire-protocol break shows up as a red test rather than a field that has
to be added after the beta cut.

## Result

**16/16 contracts verified.**

All documented broker contracts pass against the in-process broker model.

## How it runs

The suite is credential-free: it drives the `broker.Client` against the shared
broker stub (`broker/brokertest`) and small purpose-built `httptest` stubs for
the error-status contracts. It needs no secrets and no network, so it runs in
`make check` and CI on every change:

```sh
(cd cmd/probe && go test ./compat/...)   # or: make check
```

Regenerate this report after adding or changing a check:

```sh
make compat-report
```

## Coverage

| ID | Contract | Compatibility check | Result |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| C01 | §3.3 POST /session | CreateSession registers a virtual runner and returns a non-empty sessionId. | ✅ PASS |
| C02 | §3.3 POST /session (400) | A 400 version-too-old response surfaces as *broker.VersionTooOldError (non-retriable). | ✅ PASS |
| C03 | §3.3 POST /session (401/403) | 401 and 403 on CreateSession surface as *broker.UnauthorizedError (refresh-token signal). | ✅ PASS |
| C04 | §3.3 POST /session (409) | A 409 Conflict (agent already has a session) surfaces as an error the listener can act on. | ✅ PASS |
| C05 | §3.3 GET /message (202) | GetMessage returns (nil, nil) on a 202 no-job long-poll response. | ✅ PASS |
| C06 | §3.3 GET /message; §3.4 RunnerJobRequest | A queued job arrives as a RunnerJobRequest carrying run_service_url and runner_request_id. | ✅ PASS |
| C07 | §3.3 GET /message (404/410) | 404 and 410 on GetMessage surface as *broker.SessionExpiredError (recreate-session signal). | ✅ PASS |
| C08 | §3.5 GET /message (429) | 429 surfaces as *broker.RateLimitError, honoring Retry-After when present and signalling backoff when absent. | ✅ PASS |
| C09 | §3.3 run_service_url vs broker_url | acquirejob/renewjob target the per-job run_service_url; reusing the static broker_url 404s (the cached-URL pitfall). | ✅ PASS |
| C10 | §3.3/§3.4 AcquireJob x-plan-id | The x-plan-id response header takes precedence over .plan.planId; the body value is used when the header is absent. | ✅ PASS |
| C11 | §3.3/§3.4 POST /renewjob | RenewJob extends the lock and returns a future lockedUntil. | ✅ PASS |
| C12 | §3.3 session reuse (M1 Investigation C) | GetMessage on the same sessionId immediately after AcquireJob stays valid (202, no error) — no delete→create cycle. | ✅ PASS |
| C13 | §3.3 acknowledge (M1 Investigation A) | The full create→poll→acquire→renew→delete lifecycle completes with zero acknowledge calls. | ✅ PASS |
| C14 | §3.2 encryptionKey (RSA-OAEP SHA-1) | A session key wrapped with RSA-OAEP(SHA-1) — the .NET runner default — round-trips through DecryptSessionKey. | ✅ PASS |
| C15 | §3.4 encrypted message body | A body encrypted as base64(IV‖AES-256-CBC(PKCS#7)) round-trips through DecryptMessageBody into a RunnerJobRequestBody. | ✅ PASS |
| C16 | §3.3/§3.4 POST /renewjob auth | RenewJob presents the acquirejob response's SystemVssConnection AccessToken (JobAuthToken); the broker session token is rejected 401 (Q247). | ✅ PASS |

## Scope and the live probe

This suite verifies conformance to the **documented** broker contract against an
in-process model of the broker. It does not, by itself, talk to GitHub. The
behaviours the model encodes were confirmed against real GitHub during
Milestone 1 — see the live-probe findings in
the Milestone 1 plan §8 (acknowledge not required,
session reuse after acquire, egress-IP variance). The companion
credential-gated probe binary (`cmd/probe`) is the live check that the model
still matches production; this suite is the repeatable, always-green guard that
the client honours that contract.

Not covered here (requires live GitHub credentials, run via the probe binary):
the GitHub App JWT→installation-token exchange, the runner OAuth token
assertion, and GitHub's server-side minimum-runner-version threshold. These are
exercised by the credential-gated probe, not by this in-process suite.
